methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 49.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Akaer is a growth-stage space and aerospace company that has raised USD 37.7M in government funding. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace—a total of 13 observable comparables in the Space & Aerospace sector.

Two primary triangulation methods were evaluated. Peer-median benchmarking is not applicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier companies yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, insufficient for robust median calculation. Public-comp anchoring using the sector P/S median of 49.8x across the 6 public tickers is similarly not executable because Akaer's revenue has not been disclosed, as is standard for private companies at this stage.

Without revenue visibility or a sufficiently deep peer cohort meeting stage and tier criteria, a quantitative triangulation cannot be supported by the available data. Any valuation range derived from the public sector median (49.8x P/S) would require revenue disclosure and would risk over-indexing on large, mature public players. The key uncertainty blocking analysis is Akaer's current revenue or near-term revenue guidance. Prior to valuation synthesis, revenue disclosure and identification of at least 3 materially comparable private or adjacent-stage public companies would be necessary to anchor a defensible range.
